prompt: I have a DSPy RAG pipeline and I want to figure out whether my retriever or my generator is the weak link. How do I evaluate this?
expected_output: Uses Ragas with decomposed metrics (Faithfulness, ContextPrecision, ContextRecall, AnswerRelevancy) to diagnose retriever vs generator. Shows how to collect predictions, build an EvaluationDataset with SingleTurnSample, and interpret results.
assertions: Shows at least Faithfulness and one context metric
Explains which metrics evaluate the retriever vs the generator
Does not use deprecated LangchainLLMWrapper — uses llm_factory() if custom LLM needed
Mentions that ContextPrecision and ContextRecall require ground truth references

prompt: Should I use Ragas or dspy.Evaluate to measure my RAG pipeline quality? What is the difference?
expected_output: Explains that dspy.Evaluate is for overall accuracy and optimization objectives, while Ragas decomposes quality into retriever vs generator metrics. Recommends dspy.Evaluate for optimization and Ragas for post-optimization diagnosis.
assertions: Recommends dspy.Evaluate with SemanticF1 as the optimization objective
Recommends Ragas for post-optimization analysis to diagnose retriever vs generator
Warns that Ragas metrics are too slow for inner-loop optimization
Does not claim one tool replaces the other — they are complementary

prompt: I want to evaluate my RAG pipeline with Ragas but I do not have ground truth answers. Which metrics can I still use?
expected_output: Recommends Faithfulness and AnswerRelevancy as reference-free metrics. Explains that ContextPrecision, ContextRecall, and AnswerCorrectness require ground truth.
assertions: Identifies Faithfulness and AnswerRelevancy as reference-free
States that ContextPrecision, ContextRecall, and AnswerCorrectness need reference answers
Shows working code using SingleTurnSample without the reference field or with reference=None
Does not use deprecated Ragas APIs (Dataset, LangchainLLMWrapper, ground_truths)
